cards.back: A scheduling algorithm in which each process is given a fixed time slice (called a time quantum) to execute, and then the next process is given a time slice, and so on.
A scheduling algorithm in which the operating system assigns a priority to each process based on the process's priority level, and the process with the highest priority is executed first.
The ability of a computer to execute multiple processes or programs concurrently, allowing the user to perform multiple tasks simultaneously.
The use of multiple processors to execute multiple processes or programs concurrently, allowing the computer to perform multiple tasks simultaneously.
A technique used in multiprocessing to distribute workload evenly across multiple processors, ensuring that no single processor is overloaded and becomes a bottleneck.
